name: The White House
subject: President Trump agrees to meet North Korea’s Kim Jong-un
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d71b152a7392ec4275bca3e892640f2847f0767ca40c76374e11bab86e2078d6cc4f401b4744cd58153e88c2a50991c3fbf

President Trump agrees to meet North Korea’s Kim Jong-un

Speaking just outside the White House’s West Wing last night, South Korean National Security Advisor Chung Eui-Yong delivered news that President Donald J. Trump’s maximum pressure policy—together with international solidarity driven by American leadership—had brought the North Korea situation to an important juncture

“Today, I had the privilege of briefing President Trump on my recent visit to Pyongyang, North Korea,” Chung said. “In our meeting, North Korean leader Kim Jong-un said he is committed to denuclearization. Kim pledged that North Korea will refrain from any further nuclear or missile tests. He understands that the routine joint military exercises between the Republic of Korea and the United States must continue.”

Kim also expressed his eagerness to meet President Trump “as soon as possible.” The President said he would meet Kim Jong-un by May to achieve permanent denuclearization.

“Great progress being made but sanctions will remain until an agreement is reached. Meeting being planned!,” President Trump tweeted last night.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d7122232c8350caddfbddf5c9a030d678ac096977e3e3f3987459aa85ebf45a83593478e522367ca50a12252c3063fe0019
Read the full statement by Chung about North Korea.

Protecting American steel and aluminum

President Donald J. Trump is addressing unfair trade practices in the steel and aluminum industries by putting in place a 25 percent tariff on steel imports and 10 percent tariff on aluminum imports.

“Economic vitality, growth, and prosperity at home is absolutely necessary for American power and influence abroad,” the President says. “A Nation that does not protect prosperity at home cannot protect its interests abroad.”

A few key details about these Proclamations:

-

Tariffs on steel and aluminum are anticipated to reduce imports to levels needed for these American industries to achieve long-term viability.
- The strengthening of the U.S. domestic steel and aluminum industries will reduce our reliance on foreign producers.
- President Trump is welcoming any country with which we have a security relationship to discuss alternative ways to address these concerns.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d7178f83547862be70c7b0b7d906ebfad46aca1a1319c46ce7a3fb51b95d84a011191770206ba56d667107a8bf0fb7b4fec
Learn more about how President Trump is addressing unfair trade practices.

Go deeper:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d7131d8534e78fdcf06ed60d8eaea712a203e100db2721d8ca8b61bd33e78954560640169e908154c67e06ba3168ebf08f9
The President will protect national security from the effects of unfair trade.

Closing the ‘digital gender gap’

The revolution in information technology over the past few decades has often been called a “great equalizer.” Yet just as the impact from digital technology is accelerating, far too many women are being left behind. According to USAID, more than a billion and a half women in low- and middle-income countries today still do not own mobile phones.

Yesterday, as the world recognized International Women’s Day, Ivanka Trump joined with USAID Administrator Mark Green to launch USAID’s next women’s empowerment initiative, the WomenConnect Challenge. The initiative is a global call for solutions designed to close the gender digital divide, with USAID identifying and testing promising pilot approaches.

“Through closing the digital gender gap, women can help pull their families out of extreme poverty and serve as active participants and leaders in their societies,” USAID says.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d7177e32489f004912093d4ae0ef577161f68e85bdc35360413f757fe05d6c954055f8f8b484d7ba792f46b7c1d5f72447d
Learn more about the WomenConnect Challenge and the Trump Administration’s work to empower women.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump signs the Section 232 Proclamations on Steel and Aluminum Imports | March 8, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will receive his daily intelligence briefing. In the afternoon, Vice President Mike Pence will deliver keynote remarks at an America First Policies “Tax Cuts to Put America First” event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d71a4ebdb9f481e6c81a0d5ae663f59d032cc3446067fede224d9a298ab466f09bda3fd5393752f6c3dcdee0a2d1c062a01
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2da87510c1764d719cb8b88c712beaeddb102df013ad0b11e7e6f59331e4bfbb4eddae2be15da5226f9efba607e0ec9a0a7b8572fa32f12b
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4ce20c3c78f6ed7361c346e1d358c3eab52caad16b1ab9e9114db45b3304b175d5c1afc64eff1113d4bdccf79a213d0da7ec604259298d1db

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Help me take on Ted Cruz in November
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
| | |
---|---|---
| Dear Activist, Last night, we won the Texas Democratic primary in our campaign to take on Ted Cruz and represent Texas in the United States Senate.

I am grateful for the opportunity to run this race with you over the last year, and I'm looking forward to the work ahead.

Over the next eight months, I will continue to visit with Texans wherever they are, in every part of the state, in each of Texas' 254 counties -- without regard to party, geography, or anything else that might otherwise divide us. We're going to do this the right way -- taking our direction from Texans from every walk of life, to do the important, ambitious work that the country is waiting for.

[**Sign up right now to capture our momentum and join our campaign to take on Ted Cruz. It's going to take all of us, working together, to win in Texas -- but I know we have what it takes.**](http://mail.democrats.com:81/CT00161101MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2018-03-09)

Our campaign is powered by people like you. We're going to strive to ensure that any of us looking for work will be able to get the training and education that is critical to a good-paying job; to support every kid's ability to get a great public school education; to lead on immigration and the environment; to support our service members and fulfill our commitments to our veterans. And we're going to make sure that everyone can see a doctor, a therapist, and afford the medication that will allow them to lead a better life.

When we kicked off our campaign nearly one year ago, the political insiders told us we didn't stand a chance. But together, we've shown them what's possible when you reject politics as usual and put your trust completely with people. The latest polls show us within striking distance of the junior Senator from Texas, and we've already out-raised Cruz multiple times with grassroots donations alone, without taking a dollar from PACs.

We've learned that Texans turn out in record numbers when we campaign in a way that focuses on the problems and opportunities of real people - not corporations or special interests.

[**Help us keep it going. Sign up now to show you're ready to take on Ted Cruz and the PACs and the super PACs, corporations and special interests propping him up. We can do this.**](http://mail.democrats.com:81/CT00161102MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2018-03-09)

Thank you for being a partner in our big-hearted, people-powered race for Texas. We couldn't do it without you.

Sincerely,

Beto O'Rourke **[SIGN UP](http://mail.democrats.com:81/CT00161103MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2018-03-09)**
---

name: Martin Heinrich
subject: This is baffling
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE
body:
|
---

| | | Friend,

Did you know there is an amendment **banning** the Center for Disease Control and Prevention from studying how public health is impacted by gun violence?

It's true. It is called the Dickey Amendment, and it has prevented the federal government from research into gun violence's impact on America's health for over 20 years.

**Repealing the Dickey Amendment will give us the facts to tackle gun violence head on.[Will you join me and call on Congress to repeal the Dickey Amendment immediately so we can protect or children, families, and communities?](http://nm.martinheinrich. "http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/repeal-dickey?source=heinrich&redirect_url=http://sign.actiontaker.org/314-repeal-dickey-heinrich?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=1+-+Will+you+join+me+and+call+on+Congress+to&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180309_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180309_1_1")**

[](http://nm.martinheinrich. "http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/repeal-dickey?source=heinrich&redirect_url=http://sign.actiontaker.org/314-repeal-dickey-heinrich?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180309_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180309_1_1")
As an engineer, I understand how important it is to have accurate information to solve a problem.That is why I think it's baffling we still have this law on the books.

It's time we allowed the CDC to do its job and treat gun violence as what it is -- a public health crisis. With the right information, we can finally tackle this problem head on and curb gun violence in our country.

[**It 's time to finally end the prohibition against federal government researching gun violence in America. Sign on here if you agree.**](http://nm.martinheinrich. "http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/repeal-dickey?source=heinrich&redirect_url=http://sign.actiontaker.org/314-repeal-dickey-heinrich?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=87103&city=Albuquerque&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=heinrich&utm_content=3+-+Itrsquos+time+to+finally+end+the+prohibi&utm_campaign=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180309_1_1&refcode=MH_EM_PT_180DO_180309_1_1")

Thank you. With your help, I am hopeful we can end the cycle of gun violence.

\-- Martin

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Elections shouldn't be decided by dark money
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Friends, Elections shouldn't be determined by two billionaire brothers deciding to spend $400 million. **Elections in America should be determined by the people.** And while money doesn't equal votes, it can buy a whole lot of influence, whether we like it or not. The Supreme Court's decision in _Citizens United_ opened the door to an avalanche of special interest spending in our elections -- and it's degrading our democracy, bit by bit. [_**I'm calling for action to get dark money out of elections before the damage is too severe to fix. Join me by adding your name.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f95/622b7b5b/19b5397b/307bb000/1106938160/VEsH/ "http://endoutsidemoney.com/#wasserman-schultz") Thank you. Debbie

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Coming out of the freeze FAST
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The fundraising freeze is over, and we're three whole months behind. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbe/2f5ea88d/73714e32/2cbc91a0/3965716666/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fundraising-freeze-over?utm_source=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&utm_campaign=post-freeze-1&utm_medium=ema") Friend, It's time to defrost, Washington. Look, we have been hard at work since December 9th preparing for and working through the legislative session. During that entire time I've been under a fundraising freeze. That means **we're three whole months behind.** The end of the legislative session yesterday means we can finally de-ice -- and once again begin to build up our resources to keep fighting Donald Trump's dangerous agenda. **This year, it has a special urgency for me.** Across the country, states with Democratic governors and Democratic-controlled legislatures are on the frontlines of resistance against the chaos in the White House. [**I'm counting on friends like you for support. Will you rush a donation to make sure I can help strong Democrats compete and win in 2018?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbe/2f5ea88d/73714e32/2cbc91a0/3965716666/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fundraising-freeze-over?utm_source=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&utm_campaign=post-freeze-1&utm_medium=ema") As chair of the Democratic Governors Association, I'm going all out to elect Democratic governors everywhere in November: 36 seats, 36 elections, 36 chances at victory. With 26 Republican governor's seats on the ballot this November, Democrats have a real opportunity to flip big states from red to blue -- including Florida, Illinois, Nevada, Ohio, and Wisconsin. Huge victories in the Virginia and New Jersey governors' races last year set the stage for a Democratic wave around the nation this year -- but only if we join together to seize this moment. We can't take anything for granted. Republicans, and the extreme special interests that back them, are already ramping up their fundraising operations to protect vulnerable GOP governors. [**That's why I need your support today. Will you contribute immediately to help me get additional resources to exceptional Democrats running for governor in 2018?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbe/2f5ea88d/73714e32/2cbc91a0/3965716666/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fundraising-freeze-over?utm_source=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&utm_campaign=post-freeze-1&utm_medium=ema") Over the last two months of the legislative session, I'm proud of what we accomplished together. We were the first state to protect net neutrality and an open internet, regardless of what folks in D.C. are doing. We passed a ban on bump stocks that will save lives. And we fought to expand voting rights, ensuring every Washingtonian's voice is heard. But the legally mandated freeze did put us far behind where we need to be headed into these crucial months before Election Day 2018. So I appreciate your help today. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cbe/2f5ea88d/73714e32/2cbc91a0/3965716666/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fundraising-freeze-over?utm_source=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&refcode=ema_2018-03-09_post-freeze-1&utm_campaign=post-freeze-1&utm_medium=ema")

subject: Forrester Statement on Jobs Report
body:
**Forrester Statement on Jobs Report**
**For Immediate Release:** March 9, 2018**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310
CONCORD - NHGOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding the latest Jobs Report:
“The American economy is thriving under President Trump and our GOP-led Congress. Thanks to their leadership, the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act has continued to put money back in the pockets of middle-class Americans through bonuses, wage increases, and additional benefits. We are also seeing record-low unemployment, rapid job creation, and high consumer confidence. It is time that our obstructionist federal delegation put the people of New Hampshire first and work with Republicans to continue to provide economic prosperity to the American people.” ###

subject: Keep it up.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
We know it gets exhausting to constantly get emails about what we're up against. |
---

| | Peter,

We know it gets exhausting to constantly get emails about what we're up against. But we don't send them just for the sake of it -- we send them because of how critically important these issues are.

Yesterday, we emailed you about the latest attempt from the Trump administration to dismantle our health care system. This time, they're forcing through insurance plans that could eliminate coverage for people with pre-existing conditions and charge folks more when they're sick.

**Joe 's fighting back against this latest, despicable effort, and that's put a huge target on this back.**

Special interests like the Koch brothers and McConnell's super PAC have already spent more than $4M against Joe to try and replace him with a "yes man" for their extreme agenda -- and they'll spend even more to see their plan through.

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: We just made running for office a lot easier
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5116/24c69fd9/101a0e0af/18963ede/476237363/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEO24N_digital_email_20180309_TCLaunch_inact&source=WQENEO24N_digital_email_20180309_TCLaunch_inact")
---

Peter,

I'm so excited to share some major news with you about the future of EMILY's List:

We have a brand-new online training platform for candidates and enthusiastic campaign volunteers!

We've spent the last year putting our decades of knowledge into this new website where anyone, anywhere can learn the basics of how to run for office.

And now it's here. I hope you check it out and share it -- we want pro-choice Democratic women around the country to know there's a new resource for them.

**[Get your first look at the EMILY's List Training Center!](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5116/24c69fd9/101a0e0af/18963edf/476237363/VEsE/ "https://trainingcenter.emilyslist.org?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Get+your+first+look+at+the+EMILYs+List+T&utm_campaign=WQENEO24N_digital_email_20180309_TCLaunch_inact&source=WQENEO24N_digital_email_20180309_TCLaunch_inact")**

In the Training Center, candidates will find everything they need to know about running for office, including finance plan templates, best practices for working with press, tips for how to build and run an email and social media program, and much, much more.

You built this, Peter. The overwhelming support from this community over the last year in both enthusiasm and, yes, money made this new website that can train thousands of new candidates possible.

This is how, together, we turn a wave into a sea change.

**Check out the new Training Center you helped build -- I know you're going to love it:**

**[https://trainingcenter.emilyslist.org](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5116/24c69fd9/101a0e0af/18963ed8/476237363/VEsF/ "https://trainingcenter.emilyslist.org?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpstrainingcenteremilyslistorg&utm_campaign=WQENEO24N_digital_email_20180309_TCLaunch_inact&source=WQENEO24N_digital_email_20180309_TCLaunch_inact")**

I'm so glad to have you on the EMILY's List team,

Stephanie Schriock

body:

Team --
Vice President Mike Pence recently stood in front of a room of anti-abortion activists and touted the "great progress" the Trump administration has made toward rolling back access to reproductive health care.
He didn't stop there -- he claimed that the clock is rolling so far back on women's rights that **we could go back to the days before _Roe v. Wade_ and once again ban legal abortion "in our time."**
We need to fight against these attacks with everything we've got.**[Please sign our petition and tell Mike Pence and ultraconservative Republicans that denying women the right to make their own health care decisions isn't just wrong, it puts women's lives and safety on the line.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf29/2a380d31/4227403/f1e709/4176549973/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180303_ProtectReproductiveRights?source=e20180309_ProtectRR_rs&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**
The scary thing is that if we're not careful, there could be some truth to what Pence said. Toward the end of last year, House Republicans passed a ban on abortion after 20 weeks.
As long as these attacks on women's reproductive health care continue, we cannot have an equal society. So, as long as these attacks continue, we've got to do everything we can to fight back.
**One of the best ways to do that is to add your name to this petition calling on Republicans to stop undermining a woman 's right to choose. [Click here to sign.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf29/2a380d31/4227403/f1e709/4176549973/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180303_ProtectReproductiveRights?source=e20180309_ProtectRR_rs&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**

body:
I loved Black Panther. From the strong female characters to the great representation of so many elements of African culture, the film was an amazing experience.

Friend,
I loved Black Panther. From the strong female characters to the great representation of so many elements of African culture, the film was an amazing experience.
Seeing it one time wasn't enough though, and I want to take a second journey to Wakanda. Will you join me? **[Chip in $5 now for a chance to have dinner with me and see Black Panther!](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff76/767134fb/5cc48440/43382c32/681016168/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-sweepstakes?refcode=rtw_180309_sweepstakes_em")**

I want to say more, but I don't want to spoil it for you if you haven't seen the film yet!
**We 're picking eight winners to join me for Black Panther and dinner at ****Jodeem****African Cuisine in Greenbelt.[There are only a few days left to win, so chip in $5 now to enter.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff76/767134fb/5cc48440/43382c32/681016168/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-sweepstakes?refcode=rtw_180309_sweepstakes_em")**

subject: We won in Washington state -- and it’s just the start.
disclaimer_text: paid for by LegitAction, Inc
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend, breaking news!

Washington State just passed automatic voter registration (AVR) after
Legit supporters throughout the state stepped up to take action and
voice their support. Soon, millions of previously unregistered voters
will get to participate in their elections.

This much-needed victory comes at a time when our democracy is under
more strain than it has been in living memory. But it’s also a sign of
a growing movement, a movement of resistance to the assaults on our
democratic legitimacy that’s picking up steam and changing real laws
in states across the country.

Help LegitAction support this growing tide of grassroots movements --
so together we can turn the tide on the crisis of legitimacy that’s
taken over in D.C.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/5655?t=1002&akid=698%2E356062%2E_qYAEn ]Automatic voter registration just passed in Washington State with
the help of Legit members. Contribute
$3 today to help LegitAction keep
organizing for more victories like this.

Activists in New Jersey and Florida are pushing hard to restore the
ability to vote to millions of folks with a felony conviction. And
California just passed the largest expansion of youth voter access in
U.S. history.

LegitAction supporters and fellow progressives across the nation are
rejecting bogus voter fraud claims as an excuse to seize power from
the the people. We’re fighting for laws that bring more voices into
our democracy. And in Washington, we just won big.

But while we can and should celebrate victory, today we’re also
celebrating the achievement of a big milestone on the road to major,
nationwide change.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/5655?t=1003&akid=698%2E356062%2E_qYAEn ]Contribute $3 today
to help LegitAction keep building a movement for major, nationwide
change everywhere Americans are fighting for legitimacy.

Our achievement in Washington State shows why we can’t sit back and
wait for others to push for reform. Progress won’t keep happening
unless we keep giving our all to the fight.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

subject: Setting the Stage
body:
For months now, we've been telling you the story. It's a story of an United States Senator. His constituents in Indiana sent him to Washington to... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c4c04f757f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Setting the Stage_** For months now, we've been telling you a story. It's a story of a United States Senator who was sent by his constituents in Indiana to Washington to represent their values and interests. And while he's home, he sounds like he's doing just that, talking about the need to fix Obamacare and the importance of reeling in an out-of-control federal government.

But when he's in Washington, this United States Senator sings a completely different tune. He's ingrained in his big-spending, out-of-touch political party, and takes his voting cues from his party leaders instead of everyday Hoosiers. Not only that, but he said that his constituents who voted for the president [weren't "who America is."](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=52a8029296&e=1acd9bb3d6) He even voted against cutting his constituents' taxes and against fixing their broken health care system. He became so busy supporting his party, he [forgot to actually pass any legislation of his own](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6827c300d0&e=1acd9bb3d6).

Unfortunately, we all know that this isn't just a story: It's the real-life scenario facing Hoosiers today. Democrat Joe Donnelly tries to portray himself as focused on Hoosiers, but we know that on the issues that matter most, he consistently votes with Democrats like Chuck Schumer and Nancy Pelosi. And he's living in fantasy land if he thinks Hoosiers will continue to fall for the tall tale he's trying to sell us today.

We're telling this story across the state, from local Republican Lincoln Day Dinners ([check this schedule](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3dc25bb2c1&e=1acd9bb3d6) to see when your local dinner is scheduled) to [the airwaves of your local radio station](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1771a09898&e=1acd9bb3d6). We'll continue to tell this story throughout the year, so that every Hoosiers knows the truth about Democrat Joe Donnelly.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Photo: This week, Chairman Kyle Hupfer was the keynote speaker for the Newton County Lincoln Day Dinner, where he talked about the experienced leaders running on our Republican statewide ticket this year -- including Auditor Tera Klutz (pictured) and Treasurer Kelly Mitchell, who were both in attendance -- and the reasons why it's time to fire Democrat Joe Donnelly. _
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| **_Levels of Care: Helping Save Lives_** With just a few days left in this year's legislative session, this week **Governor Eric Holcomb** signed a major bill -- a key part of his [2018 Next Level Agenda](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=de6f601eb2&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- which will play a big role in saving the lives of mothers and babies throughout Indiana.

Known as Levels of Care, [this new state law](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2e9dbbd4ee&e=1acd9bb3d6) standardizes the way hospitals and birthing facilities across the state are rated, making sure that mothers are delivering their babies in facilities that are equipped to meet their specific needs. By using a set standard, Hoosiers will face less confusion when comparing facilities \-- in turn lowering infant mortality rates, lowering health care costs and ensuring the best possible start of life for all new Hoosiers.

This new law is another step toward Governor Holcomb's pledge to make Indiana home to the lowest infant mortality rates in the nation by 2024. As Governor Holcomb said, "We know we can’t fix our state’s infant mortality problem overnight, but this bill will help us save Hoosier lives."
---
|
---

His constituents in Indiana sent him to Washington to... | | | | | | | Setting the Stage For months now, we've been telling you a story. It's a story of a United States Senator who was sent by his constituents in Indiana to Washington to represent their values and interests. And while he's home, he sounds like he's doing just that, talking about the need to fix Obamacare and the importance of reeling in an out-of-control federal government. But when he's in Washington, this United States Senator sings a completely different tune. He's ingrained in his big-spending, out-of-touch political party, and takes his voting cues from his party leaders instead of everyday Hoosiers. Not only that, but he said that his constituents who voted for the president He even voted against cutting his constituents' taxes and against fixing their broken health care system. He became so busy supporting his party, he . Unfortunately, we all know that this isn't just a story: It's the real-life scenario facing Hoosiers today. Democrat Joe Donnelly tries to portray himself as focused on Hoosiers, but we know that on the issues that matter most, he consistently votes with Democrats like Chuck Schumer and Nancy Pelosi. And he's living in fantasy land if he thinks Hoosiers will continue to fall for the tall tale he's trying to sell us today. We're telling this story across the state, from local Republican Lincoln Day Dinners ( to see when your local dinner is scheduled) to . We'll continue to tell this story throughout the year, so that every Hoosiers knows the truth about Democrat Joe Donnelly. -Kyle Hupfer Photo: This week, Chairman Kyle Hupfer was the keynote speaker for the Newton County Lincoln Day Dinner, where he talked about the experienced leaders running on our Republican statewide ticket this year -- including Auditor Tera Klutz (pictured) and Treasurer Kelly Mitchell, who were both in attendance -- and the reasons why it's time to fire Democrat Joe Donnelly. | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Levels of Care: Helping Save Lives With just a few days left in this year's legislative session, this week Governor Eric Holcomb signed a major bill -- a key part of his \-- which will play a big role in saving the lives of mothers and babies throughout Indiana. Known as Levels of Care, standardizes the way hospitals and birthing facilities across the state are rated, making sure that mothers are delivering their babies in facilities that are equipped to meet their specific needs. By using a set standard, Hoosiers will face less confusion when comparing facilities \-- in turn lowering infant mortality rates, lowering health care costs and ensuring the best possible start of life for all new Hoosiers. This new law is another step toward Governor Holcomb's pledge to make Indiana home to the lowest infant mortality rates in the nation by 2024. As Governor Holcomb said, "We know we can’t fix our state’s infant mortality problem overnight, but this bill will help us save Hoosier lives." | | Upcoming Republican Events \- Warren Twp Lincoln Day **WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, MARCH 9TH**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**"Neither snow nor rain nor heat nor gloom of night stays these couriers from the swift completion of their appointed rounds." I was reminded of the popular pledge this past week when the Governor, House, and Senate refused to be deterred by stormy weather in our state.**
****
**The Governor was on top of the snow storm situation at all times and also managed to get a unanimous confirmation of Associate Justice Bob Lynn as the new Chief Justice of the New Hampshire Supreme Court.**
****
**In the House and Senate, a flurry of bills were voted on: **
****
**NO NEW TAXES – Thank a Republican!**

**Senator French issued a statement praising the Ways & Means Committee’s recommendation to kill a bill proposed by the Democrats to raise car registration fees, saying “It is important for New Hampshire legislators to protect taxpayers from unnecessary taxes...” No new taxes? Thank a Republican! If the Democrats had been in control, you can be sure that tax would have passed!**
****
**REDUCING REGULATION – Thank a Republican!**

**The House passed a bill establishing a statutory commission for oversight over occupational regulation. This commission will assure boards utilize the least restrictive regulation and require continuing review of all occupational boards. House Majority Leader Hinch said, “This was an important first step in knocking down barriers blocking average Granite Staters from finding work.” Want less regulation and a better business environment? Go ahead – Thank a Republican!! Rest assured that the Democrats would have created a commission designed to create MORE regulation!**
****
**PARENTS GET TO DECIDE – Thank a Republican!**

**The Senate Education Committee killed a bill that exempted certain non-academic surveys from having a consent requirement. Said Senator Avard after the vote, “Just last session we gave parents the option to decide if their children will participate in certain non-academic surveys which can be overly intrusive into family’s private lives. This legislation would have reversed these protections, forcing children to take these surveys without their parents’ knowledge or consent. I am pleased that the NH Senate agrees that parents have the right to decide what personal information their children divulge and that private family matters should stay that way.” So, do you think a Democrat would let a parent have that kind of control—don’t think so. So, go ahead -- Thank a Republican!!!**
****
**Republicans are getting the job done in New Hampshire! We ARE headed in the right direction. We enjoy:**
****
* **Being the #1 state to raise a child**

* **Having the lowest poverty rate in the nation**

* **Being #1 best state for opportunity**

* **The highest median income in the country**

* **Being the #1 state for economic freedom**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, MARCH 9TH | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, "Neither snow nor rain nor heat nor gloom of night stays these couriers from the swift completion of their appointed rounds." I was reminded of the popular pledge this past week when the Governor, House, and Senate refused to be deterred by stormy weather in our state. ** The Governor was on top of the snow storm situation at all times and also managed to get a unanimous confirmation of Associate Justice Bob Lynn as the new Chief Justice of the New Hampshire Supreme Court. In the House and Senate, a flurry of bills were voted on: NO NEW TAXES – Thank a Republican! Senator French issued a statement praising the Ways & Means Committee’s recommendation to kill a bill proposed by the Democrats to raise car registration fees, saying “It is important for New Hampshire legislators to protect taxpayers from unnecessary taxes...” No new taxes? Thank a Republican! If the Democrats had been in control, you can be sure that tax would have passed! REDUCING REGULATION – Thank a Republican! The House passed a bill establishing a statutory commission for oversight over occupational regulation. This commission will assure boards utilize the least restrictive regulation and require continuing review of all occupational boards. House Majority Leader Hinch said, “This was an important first step in knocking down barriers blocking average Granite Staters from finding work.” Want less regulation and a better business environment? Go ahead – Thank a Republican!! Rest assured that the Democrats would have created a commission designed to create MORE regulation! PARENTS GET TO DECIDE – Thank a Republican! The Senate Education Committee killed a bill that exempted certain non-academic surveys from having a consent requirement. Said Senator Avard after the vote, “Just last session we gave parents the option to decide if their children will participate in certain non-academic surveys which can be overly intrusive into family’s private lives. This legislation would have reversed these protections, forcing children to take these surveys without their parents’ knowledge or consent. I am pleased that the NH Senate agrees that parents have the right to decide what personal information their children divulge and that private family matters should stay that way.” So, do you think a Democrat would let a parent have that kind of control—don’t think so. So, go ahead -- Thank a Republican!!! Republicans are getting the job done in New Hampshire! We ARE headed in the right direction. We enjoy: Being the #1 state to raise a child Having the lowest poverty rate in the nation Being #1 best state for opportunity The highest median income in the country Being the #1 state for economic freedom Having the fastest growing economy in New England So take a minute folks, and thank your, , or the today! body:
Last year, the people of Southeast Missouri came together with one voice to say: we don't want welfare.

Last year, the people of Southeast Missouri came together with one voice to say: we don't want welfare. We want to work. Bring back American jobs.

After a special session and a lot of work by people all over the state and country, I was proud to be back in the bootheel today to announce that an aluminum smelter is opening with 450 quality jobs at an average salary of $64,000!
